purpose: Conformance vectors for per-chunk retrieval provenance under action-ref-v1. Per-chunk provenance rides inside the signed retriever receipt (no change to action_ref, which stays component-level). A conformant trace lets an independent verifier map every generator claim back to a specific span in a specific document. Each fail-closed vector is a way that property breaks.
source_hash_definition: source_hash = "sha256:" + hex(SHA-256(JCS({bbox, chunk_id, doc_id, page, text})))

error_codes.SOURCE_HASH_MISMATCH: Presented span does not recompute to the embedded source_hash under JCS or colon-join — content altered after signing.
error_codes.NONCANONICAL_DERIVATION: source_hash recomputes under colon-joined string but not under JCS; derivation is not portable across honest implementations.
error_codes.UNANCHORED_CLAIM: A generator claim cites a chunk_id absent from the retriever receipt — claim has no traceable provenance.

vectors.name: conformant_per_chunk_trace
vectors.description: Retriever receipt carries retrieved_chunks with JCS-canonical source_hash per span; the generator output cites only chunk_ids present in the receipt. Every claim is traceable to a specific span — the defensibility test holds for an independent verifier.

retrieved_chunks.text: Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days written notice.
Liability under this Agreement shall not exceed fees paid in the prior twelve months.

claims.text: The contract allows termination on 30 days notice.
Liability is capped at trailing twelve-month fees.

vectors.name: tampered_span_mismatch
vectors.description: The presented chunk text was altered after the receipt was signed (30 -> 90 days). The embedded source_hash, derived over the original span, no longer recomputes over the presented span — and does not match a colon-joined derivation either. Content tampering, not a derivation choice.

retrieved_chunks.text: Either party may terminate this Agreement upon ninety (90) days written notice.

vectors.name: noncanonical_colon_join
vectors.description: The source_hash was derived by colon-joining the span fields into a string before hashing, instead of JCS (RFC 8785) over the span object. It recomputes under the colon-joined form but not under JCS — so two honest implementations canonicalizing the same span produce different hashes, and an independent verifier cannot reproduce the trace. The verifier proves non-canonical derivation by recomputing both forms.

retrieved_chunks.text: Liability under this Agreement shall not exceed fees paid in the prior twelve months.

vectors.name: unanchored_generator_claim
vectors.description: The generator emits a claim citing chunk_id c-9999, which is not present in the retriever receipt's retrieved_chunks. The claim has no provenance — it fails the defensibility test 'every answer traceable to a specific source passage'. All present chunks have valid JCS source_hashes, so the only failure is the orphaned claim.

retrieved_chunks.text: Either party may terminate this Agreement upon thirty (30) days written notice.

claims.text: The governing law is New York.
